newsSince: No confirmed new material order since June 9. The stock saw a large volume surge and +14.7% move, but searches surface only a June 3 volume spike and prior-cycle BSNL/Bharatnet/NEC wins; the FY2026 Q1 revenue collapse (down ~87% YoY on PO timing delays) remains the salient fundamental backdrop. Move appears flow/momentum-driven.

sources.label: Tejas Networks volume surge / buy signals (MarketsMojo)

verdicts.rankRationale: Test-time-scaling lens via sovereign telecom build-outs: no confirmed new order since June 9, and the +14.7% pop looks flow/volume-driven, not a fresh fundamental win. Lumpy BSNL/Bharatnet revenue and recent ~87% YoY revenue collapse on PO timing keep conviction low. Hold at 39.
verdicts.bull: Sovereign India telecom build-out (BSNL 4G/5G, Bharatnet 3) and NEC Massive MIMO give a domestic-supply tailwind in compute-factory networking.
verdicts.neutral: Networking-equipment exposure to sovereign build-outs, but only loosely tied to the AI test-time-scaling thesis.
verdicts.bear: Severe revenue lumpiness and PO-timing-driven shortfalls; recent quarter swung to a loss, and the rally lacks a confirmed fresh catalyst.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: BSNL expansion / Bharatnet Phase 3 order conversion
verdicts.primaryRisk: Order-timing volatility and concentrated government-customer dependence
